title: Alphabet Drops 6% on Soaring AI Capex Despite 82% Cloud Surge; Meta Platforms, Snap Follow
excerpt: Alphabet just posted a cloud quarter that should have sent shares soaring, yet investors are selling hard this morning. The reason comes down to a spending c...
content_html:
Quick Read

  • Alphabet (GOOGL) dropped 6% as a capex hike to $205 billion erased the impact of its 82% Cloud surge, dragging Meta Platforms (META) and Snap (SNAP) lower too.

  • Cantor Fitzgerald and Raymond James both cut their Alphabet price targets but maintained bullish ratings, with the SOCL ETF offering diversified exposure to the group.

  • Act now: the analyst who called NVIDIA in 2010 just named his top 10 AI stocks — and Google didn't make the cut. Grab the names FREE today.

Alphabet Class A (NASDAQ:GOOGL) and Class C (NASDAQ:GOOG) shares are down 6% in Thursday morning trading, with GOOGL stock at $321.65 and GOOG stock at $320.76. The slide follows Alphabet's Q2 2026 report, released after Wednesday's close.

The drop caps a rough week for Alphabet (GOOGL) stock after a 79% one-year run. The broader tape isn't helping, with the NASDAQ 100 lower by 1.36% this morning.

Capex Guidance Hike Overshadows Cloud Blowout

[stock_chart symbol="NASDAQ:GOOGL"]

Alphabet's headline numbers were strong. The company's total revenue reached $119.8 billion, up 24% year over year, while Google Cloud revenue accelerated to $24.77 billion, up 82%. Google noted 950 million monthly active users on Gemini and a Cloud backlog of around $514 billion.

The issue here is spending. Alphabet raised its full-year 2026 capital expenditure guidance to $195 billion to $205 billion, up from $180 billion to $190 billion, and signaled that capex will rise again in 2027. Reported EPS of $9.11 was heavily inflated by a roughly $99 billion equity-securities gain, so this wasn't a clean operating blowout. Furthermore, Alphabet's Q2 free cash flow came in at -$5.9 billion.

Analyst Targets Trimmed, Ratings Held

Sell-side notes echoed the mixed message. Cantor Fitzgerald lowered its Alphabet (GOOGL) stock price target to $420 from $435, keeping an Overweight rating, citing a strong quarter with Google Cloud Platform outperforming and expanding margins, offset by higher AI infrastructure spending and limited EBIT upside.

Act now: the analyst who called NVIDIA in 2010 just named his top 10 AI stocks — and Google didn't make the cut. Grab the names FREE today.

Meanwhile, Raymond James cut its GOOG price target to $400 from $425 while maintaining Strong Buy, calling results largely in line, with YouTube and Cloud outperforming and Search slightly weaker. The firm's focus shifts to Gemini 4 performance and future model development. The pattern is telling: trimmed targets, maintained bullish ratings.

Peers Drift Lower on Broad Tech Weakness

content_html_zh:
快速阅读

  • Alphabet (GOOGL) 下跌 6%，资本支出上涨至 2050 亿美元，消除了其 82% 云计算激增的影响，也拖累 Meta Platforms (META) 和 Snap (SNAP) 走低。
  • 坎托·菲茨杰拉德 (Cantor Fitzgerald) 和雷蒙德·詹姆斯 (Raymond James) 均下调了 Alphabet 的价格目标，但维持看涨评级，SOCL ETF 为该集团提供了多元化的投资。
  • 立即行动：2010 年给 NVIDIA 打电话的分析师刚刚列出了他心目中的十大人工智能股票，而谷歌却没有上榜。 今天免费获取名字。

Alphabet A 类（纳斯达克股票代码：GOOGL）和 C 类股票（纳斯达克股票代码：GOOG）股价在周四早盘交易中下跌 6%，GOOGL 股价为 321.65 美元，GOOG 股价为 320.76 美元。 在此之前，Alphabet 在周三收盘后发布了 2026 年第二季度报告。

Alphabet (GOOGL) 股票在一年内上涨了 79% 后，此次下跌为该公司的艰难一周画上句号。 大盘也无济于事，纳斯达克 100 指数今早下跌 1.36%。

资本支出指导上调掩盖了云井喷

[stock_chart symbol="纳斯达克股票代码：GOOGL"]

Alphabet 的整体数据强劲。 该公司总收入达到 1198 亿美元，同比增长 24%，而谷歌云收入则加速至 247.7 亿美元，同比增长 82%。 谷歌指出，Gemini 每月有 9.5 亿活跃用户，云积压金额约为 5140 亿美元。

这里的问题是支出。 Alphabet 将 2026 年全年资本支出指引从 1800 亿美元至 1900 亿美元上调至 1950 亿美元至 2050 亿美元，并暗示资本支出将在 2027 年再次上升。 报告的每股收益为 9.11 美元，但由于大约 990 亿美元的股票证券收益而大幅膨胀，因此这并不是一次彻底的经营井喷。 此外，Alphabet 第二季度自由现金流为 -59 亿美元。

分析师目标下调，评级不变

卖方的笔记呼应了这一复杂的信息。 Cantor Fitzgerald 将 Alphabet (GOOGL) 股价目标从 435 美元下调至 420 美元，维持增持评级，理由是谷歌云平台表现强劲且利润率不断扩大，但人工智能基础设施支出增加和息税前利润上涨有限所抵消。

立即行动：2010 年给 NVIDIA 打电话的分析师刚刚列出了他心目中的十大人工智能股票，而谷歌却没有上榜。 今天免费获取名字。

与此同时，雷蒙德·詹姆斯 (Raymond James) 将 GOOG 目标价从 425 美元下调至 400 美元，同时维持强力买入，称结果基本符合，YouTube 和 Cloud 表现优于，而 Search 稍弱。 该公司的重点转向 Gemini 4 性能和未来模型开发。 这种模式很能说明问题：削减目标，维持看涨评级。

由于广泛的技术疲软，同行纷纷走低
